summary: Install-time credential and crypto-wallet stealer exfiltrating to hardcoded C2
details: The package impersonates a Polygon SDK but ships no library code — package.json declares `main: postinstall.js` with an empty description, and both `preinstall` and `postinstall` lifecycle hooks execute the same script, so a plain `npm install` runs the payload with no user action. postinstall.js walks the installer's home, Desktop, Documents, Downloads, /root, /tmp, and OneDrive directories and harvests wallet artifacts (id.json, wallet.json, keystore.json, metamask.json, phantom.json, seed.txt, mnemonic.txt), Solana/Ethereum keystores, `~/.ssh/id_*` and deploy keys, `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.git-credentials`, `~/.netrc`, `~/.npmrc`, `.env` files, and Chrome/Brave/Edge MetaMask and Phantom extension local storage. It also scans.txt/.md/.rtf/.csv notes for BIP39 seed-phrase content by keyword and wordlist match and uploads up to 5000 characters of any hit. Every payload is POSTed to `http://107.161.90.180:7777` with `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, and `process.cwd()` attached for attacker attribution, and an `active` beacon is sent on completion.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
